etymology.description: 雔 (two birds) + 又 (hand) -> Represents two birds in a hand
etymology.interpretation: The character 雙 (shuāng, "pair") illustrates the concept by depicting two birds held together, symbolizing a pair, with a hand component indicating possession or holding. This construction conveys the idea of pairing or doubling.
etymology.simplification: 双 (simplified from 䨇, a variant of 雙) eliminated the bird component and uses abstract duplication with the character resembling hands, reflecting a simplified representation of a pair.

examples.chinese: 我买了一双鞋子(我買了一雙鞋子)。
examples.english: I bought a pair of shoes.

examples.chinese: 这双筷子很漂亮(這雙筷子很漂亮)。
examples.english: This pair of chopsticks is very beautiful.

examples.chinese: 他有一双聪明的眼睛(他有一雙聰明的眼睛)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yī shuāng cōngmíng de yǎnjīng.
examples.english: He has a pair of intelligent eyes.
